group_label: Compounding growth-rate exponents and technology parameters
help: Exponential growth exponents compounding over a 51-year horizon and the hardware efficiency doubling proxy. These are true uncertainty priors on trajectory-shaping parameters: the prior width reflects genuine scenario disagreement about future adoption, grid decarbonization, and technology progress rates. LOW halves sigmas and tightens bounds; MEDIUM reproduces the paper-safe ensemble; HIGH is exploratory. Ohio-specific means and supports reflect Ohio's documented slower BEV-adoption and fossil-heavy grid.

parameters.label: Annual BEV-share growth exponent
parameters.physical_meaning: Annual compounding exponent of the BEV share growth. Bounded: the share cannot fall below zero or exceed 1.0 by construction. Distribution is truncated normal with explicit min/max bounds to enforce this. California AFDC 2019-2024 BEV-registration CAGR ~45%; Ohio ~30%, supporting the regional mean differentiation.
parameters.citation: DOE AFDC, State Vehicle Registration Counts (https://afdc.energy.gov/vehicle-registration). State CAGR estimated from 2019-2024 AFDC annual counts.
parameters.why_default_free: 51-year compounding is a core trajectory-shaping uncertainty. Genuine scenario disagreement about electrification pace.
parameters.region_note: Ohio mean 0.055 (vs CA 0.07) from DOE AFDC 2019-2024 registration CAGR. Ohio sd wider to reflect deeper scenario disagreement for a state more dependent on federal EV incentives than California's state-level mandates.
parameters.duplicates: partial overlap with F02 (initial BEV share, which is absorbed within 3-5 years)

parameters.label: Annual low-carbon-electricity growth exponent
parameters.physical_meaning: Annual compounding exponent of the low-carbon electricity share growth. Bounded: the share cannot exceed 1.0 by construction. Distribution is truncated normal. California has aggressive state-level targets (SB 100: 100% clean by 2045); Ohio has no equivalent statewide mandate, and 2019-2024 EIA data shows Ohio's low-carbon share rising from ~22% to ~27% (~4% CAGR) vs California's faster trajectory.
parameters.citation: EIA, State Electricity Profiles. California: https://www.eia.gov/state/print.php?sid=CA; Ohio: https://www.eia.gov/state/analysis.php?sid=OH
parameters.why_default_free: Primary interpretation-boundary driver. Genuine scenario disagreement about grid decarbonization pace.
parameters.region_note: Ohio mean 0.035 (vs CA 0.05): Ohio's fossil-heavy grid and absence of a 100%-clean mandate mean slower decarbonization. 2019-2024 EIA data supports the differentiation.
parameters.duplicates: partial overlap with F01 (initial f_clean, which is absorbed within 3-5 years)

parameters.label: Hardware efficiency doubling time (years)
parameters.physical_meaning: Fleet-level effective compute-efficiency proxy: years for the average ECAV computing energy per vehicle to halve. This aggregates hardware architecture progress (GPU/ASIC generational improvement), platform shifts (centralized compute vs distributed sensors), and software/system co-optimization (model compression, edge inference). It is NOT a vendor-specific roadmap prediction. Reparameterized as a lognormal on the doubling time to respect the positive-definite constraint (doubling time cannot be negative).
parameters.citation: No single external source for the combined fleet-level proxy. The 2.8-year arithmetic mean is consistent with historical GPU compute-per-watt doubling rates observed in NVIDIA/AMD datacenter GPU generations (2016-2024), applied here as a fleet-effective aggregate, not a chip-level roadmap.
parameters.why_default_free: Top turning-year destabilizer (16-year spread in isolated MC). Asymmetric uncertainty is physically real: slower-than-Moore scenarios (longer doubling time) are plausible; faster-than-Moore is bounded by physics.
parameters.region_note: No region-specific treatment; semiconductor technology progress is global.
parameters.design_note: Reparameterized from triangular to lognormal. The JSON key 'mean' denotes the arithmetic mean on the original (years) scale; the sampler converts via mu=ln(mean)-0.5*sigma^2. For MEDIUM (mean=2.8, sigma=0.30): mu=0.985, median=2.68yr, mode=2.45yr, p05=1.63yr, p95=4.39yr. The positive-definite right-skewed shape reflects that slow-down scenarios (longer doubling time) are more plausible than impossibly-fast ones.

parameters.label: Hardware deployment lag (years)
parameters.physical_meaning: Time gap between frontier compute-efficiency improvement and realized in-fleet installation on vehicles and STI. Applied as a time shift on the realized hardware-improvement window; does not modify the frontier doubling time (F27). A cohort added in year y receives the frontier efficiency state from year (y - F29), not year y.
parameters.citation: Expert prior / scenario assumption. No single literature source pins the fleet-integration delay; qualification, procurement, and fleet turnover each contribute.
parameters.why_default_free: Hardware deployment lag is a distinct L3 epistemic factor: qualification, integration, procurement, installation, and fleet turnover take time. Holding it at zero would implicitly assume instant frontier realization, which is not defensible.
parameters.region_note: No region-specific treatment; fleet-integration delays are a global deployment phenomenon.
parameters.design_note: Scenario lever treated independently of F27. Default 2.0 years. U(1, 4) prior for scenario-envelope sampling.

parameters.label: Annual fleet-stock growth exponent
parameters.physical_meaning: Demographically-bounded annual fleet growth rate. California ~0.2% growth/year; Ohio ~0.1% growth/year, reflecting population and registration trends.
parameters.citation: FHWA Highway Statistics series, Table MV-1 (state motor-vehicle registrations). DOE AFDC state registration counts for cross-check.
parameters.why_default_free: Demographically bounded but controls absolute fleet level.
parameters.why_medium_rejected: MEDIUM sd=0.001 was simply doubled LOW without evidence. Census-based projections do not support wider sd.
parameters.region_note: Ohio mean 0.001 (vs CA 0.002) reflecting Ohio's slower population growth (FHWA MV-1).
